fundamentals.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Spotify Technology S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides audio streaming subscription services worldwide. It operates in two segments, Premium and Ad-Supported. The Premium segment offers online and offline streaming access to its catalog of music and podcasts, including video, lossless music, and audiobooks in select markets through subscription offerings primarily sold directly to end users and partners. The Ad-Supported segment provides limited on-demand online access to its catalog of music and online and offline access to its catalog of podcasts on computers, tablets, mobile devices, and other smart devices. The company also offers sales, distribution and marketing, contract research and development, and customer and other support services. Spotify Technology S.A. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

judgment.summary: Strong growth and profitability metrics are somewhat offset by a high debt-to-equity ratio and a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 18.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Revenue growth 8.2% YoY, with strong analyst estimates for current Q (769.9%) and FY (22.4%).
Earnings growth 222.4% YoY, with quarterly earnings growth at 220.4%.
Profit margin 15.4% and Return on Equity 38.0% indicate solid operational efficiency.
Free cash flow of $707.75M TTM and a healthy current ratio of 2.06.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $607.36.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 5.94 is high, suggesting significant leverage.
Forward P/E of 28.16 and PEG ratio of 1.79 indicate a premium valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 6.11 is also on the higher side.
Beta of 1.55 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Spotify is experiencing significant positive momentum, up 15.6% since its last earnings report, which featured an 18% EPS beat. Recent attention has focused on its AI remix collaboration with Universal Music Group, alongside discussions of its potential as a Steve Cohen investment. Despite a recent dip and a 'still overvalued' note from GuruFocus, the overall sentiment is bullish, supported by strong analyst growth estimates and a consensus 'buy' rating.
